title: Condensate Drain Assembly
description: Assemble the condensate drain components in the correct order from the evaporator coil drain pan to the floor drain.
explanation: The P-trap must be immediately after the drain pan connection to create a water seal preventing conditioned air from being pulled through the drain. The air gap at termination prevents sewer gas entry per IMC 307.2.

title: Gas Pipe to Furnace Connection
description: Assemble the gas piping components from the branch line to the furnace gas valve in the correct order.
explanation: Gas pipe connections require a manual shutoff, sediment trap (drip leg) to catch debris before the gas valve, and a union for service access. Order is critical for code compliance per IFGC 408.4.

title: Thermostat Wire Run
description: Assemble the thermostat wiring path from the HVAC equipment to the wall thermostat.
explanation: Thermostat wiring uses low-voltage Class 2 wire (typically 18/5 or 18/8). Wire passing through a fire-rated assembly needs a fire-rated penetration seal. Runs must be separated from line voltage per NEC 725.

title: Refrigerant Line Set Assembly
description: Assemble the refrigerant line set components from the outdoor condensing unit to the indoor evaporator coil.

parts.name: Insulated suction line and liquid line
Wall penetration with sleeve and seal
Indoor coil flare or brazed connections

explanation: Refrigerant line installation follows a specific sequence: connect at outdoor unit, run insulated lines, seal wall penetration, make indoor connections, then pressure test with nitrogen before evacuation per IMC 1104.

title: Exhaust Fan Duct Assembly
description: Assemble the bathroom exhaust fan duct run from the fan unit to the exterior termination.

parts.name: Roof or wall cap with damper

explanation: Exhaust fan ducts must terminate outdoors — never into an attic or soffit. A short flex duct section isolates vibration, rigid duct provides the main run, and the cap with damper prevents backdraft per IRC M1507.

title: VAV Box Installation Assembly
description: Assemble the VAV terminal box and its connections in the correct order from the main duct to the diffuser.
explanation: VAV box assembly flows from the main duct through a flex connector (vibration isolation), the VAV box (flow control), reheat coil (zone heating), sound lining (noise control), then to the diffuser per ASHRAE and SMACNA standards.

title: Hot Water Reheat Coil Piping
description: Assemble the hot water piping components at a reheat coil in the correct order from the supply main to the return main.
explanation: Reheat coil piping requires isolation valves for service, a control valve on the supply side for modulating flow, and a balancing valve on the return for commissioning. This arrangement follows ASHRAE Handbook recommendations.

title: Flex Duct to Boot Connection
description: Assemble the flexible duct connection from a sheet metal branch takeoff to a ceiling supply boot.

parts.name: Flex duct inner liner over collar
Flex duct outer jacket and insulation

explanation: Proper flex duct connections require pulling the inner liner over the collar, clamping it, then sealing the outer jacket separately. Duct tape alone is NOT acceptable — mechanical clamps and mastic are required per SMACNA standards.

title: Chilled Water Coil Piping
description: Assemble the chilled water piping at an AHU cooling coil from supply to return, including all required accessories.
explanation: Chilled water coil piping follows a specific sequence: shutoff valve, strainer (protects control valve), control valve (modulates flow), coil, balancing valve (for commissioning), and return shutoff per ASHRAE 90.1.

title: Smoke Damper Assembly in Fire Wall
description: Assemble the components for a smoke damper installation where ductwork penetrates a 2-hour fire-rated wall.

parts.name: Damper actuator wired to fire alarm
Access door for inspection and testing

explanation: Fire/smoke damper installations must include a proper wall sleeve, UL-listed perimeter seal, connection to the fire alarm system for automatic closure, and an access door for required periodic testing per NFPA 80 and IBC 717.

title: Supply Register Installation
description: Assemble the components for installing a ceiling supply register from the duct branch to the finished ceiling.

parts.name: Boot sealed and secured to framing

explanation: Supply register installation starts with the boot positioned at the opening, connected to ductwork, sealed and secured to prevent rattling, then the register face is installed last. The register damper allows airflow adjustment per SMACNA standards.

title: Kitchen Hood Duct Assembly
description: Assemble the kitchen hood exhaust duct system from the hood to the rooftop exhaust fan.

parts.name: Hood exhaust collar with grease gutter
Cleanout access door at direction change
Grease duct through roof with fire-rated enclosure
Upblast exhaust fan with grease containment

explanation: Kitchen hood grease ducts must be welded steel (no flex duct or galvanized allowed), with cleanout access at every direction change, fire-rated roof penetration, and an upblast fan for proper grease containment per IMC 506 and NFPA 96.
